subject: Gaming
title: Toxicity and Online Disinhibition
description: A quick, practical guide to Toxicity and Online Disinhibition.

hook.text: People say things in game chat they'd never say face-to-face. Anonymity changes human behavior.

buildup.text: John Suler's online disinhibition effect explains why anonymity removes social filters.

discovery.text: Competitive games amplify toxicity. Losing triggers frustration, and anonymous teammates become targets.

twist.text: Riot Games found that most toxic players aren't consistently toxic—bad days create bad behavior.

climax.text: Solutions include behavioral systems, positive reinforcement, and making player identity matter.

punchline.text: Anonymity removes filters. Nice people turn toxic under pressure.

quiz.question: What causes toxicity in online gaming according to psychology?
options: Anonymity removes social filters through online disinhibition
